sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section III, List of Answers on Terminology 19:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section III, List of Answers on Terminology 19:1
items.html: Phase three, called Zeir Anpin, is called flesh. This appellation pertains in it only to the ten Sefirot whose level is even. These ten phases are called OBGAM [acronym for Or, Bassar, Gidin, Atzamot, Mochin]. Malchut is called Or (skin), ZA is called Bassar (flesh), Bina is called Gidin (Aramaic: tendons), Hochma is called Atzamot (bones), and Keter is called Mochin (marrow). (Part 3, Chapter 5, item 3)
